chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – Wearing the silk and the brocade

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/335)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/335)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ قِيَامَا أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الثَّوْبِ الْمُلْحَمِ بِالْقَزِّ وَ الْقُطْنِ وَ الْقَزُّ أَكْثَرُ مِنَ النِّصْفِ أَ يُصَلَّى فِيهِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ وَ قَدْ كَانَ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) مِنْهُ جِبَابٌ كَذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr who said,

‘Al-Hassan Bin Qiyama asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about the clothes seamed with the Al-Qazz (silk), and the cotton, and Al-Qazz (silk) is more than the half, can one Pray in it?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem’. - and Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> used to have gowns from it like that’.
en.sarwar: 11. it is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “Al-Hassan ibn Qiyaman once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a garment with its weft of al-Qazz and cotton in which al-Qazz is more than half: if one can use for Salat (prayer). He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not unlawful.’ Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, had such a gown.’”
